title: Evangelion Unit-03
wikitext:
'''NGE''' [[Evangelion Unit-03 (NTE)|NTE]] [[Evangelion Unit-03 (Anima)|Anima]] [[Evangelion Unit-03 (Other)|Other]] {{Evangelion Infobox | name = Evangelion Unit-03 | image = [[Image:18 C212 eva03.jpg|260px|Eva-03]] | japanese = エヴァンゲリオン３号機 | model_type = Production | pilots = '''Series:''' [[Toji Suzuhara]] | soul = '''Series:''' Toji's mother | first_sortie = '''Series:''' [[Episode 18]] (activation test) }} '''Evangelion Unit-03''' (エヴァンゲリオン３号機, ''Evangerion Sangouki''), built by [[Nerv]]'s [[Nerv 1st Branch|First Branch]] in the [[United States]], is the second Production Model Evangelion. ==Profile== Following the incident resulting in the sudden disappearance of the [[Nerv 2nd Branch|Second Branch]] (Nevada) and Eva-03's sister unit, [[Evangelion Unit-04|Eva-04]], the U.S. government panics and demands that Eva-03 be transported off of its soil to [[Japan]]. [[Toji Suzuhara]] is selected to serve as the Eva's pilot and sent to Nerv's secondary testing facility at Matsushiro to conduct Eva-03's first activation test, supervised by [[Ritsuko Akagi]] and [[Misato Katsuragi]]. [[Image:18 C134 bardiel.jpg|thumb|Bardiel reveals itself at Eva-03's activation test.|left]] While suspended from a transport plane during its flight to Japan, Eva-03 enters a series of cumulonimbus clouds, suspected to be the point at which it was infected by the 13th Angel, [[Bardiel]]"Conceivably, Bardiel lurked within the cumulonimbus clouds and parasitized the in-transit Eva-03 when it passed through." (積乱雲の中に潜み、移送中の３号機が通過した際に寄生したものと考えられるバルディエル。) From ''[[Evangelion Chronicle]]'', Mechanic Sheet 04: Evangelion Production Model / Thirteenth Angel Bardiel. Translation by [[User:Reichu|Reichu]].. During the activation experiment at the Matsushiro facility, Eva-03 goes [[berserk]] under, ostensibly, Bardiel's control, the parasitic "infection" manifest beneath the armor as slime fungus-like growths"Bardiel: The slime fungus-shaped Thirteenth Angel that parasitized Eva-03." (バルディエル：3号機に寄生した粘菌状の第十三使徒。) ''Neon Genesis Evangelion Newtype 100% Collection'', p. 144. Translation by Reichu.. After briefly struggling to break free from its restraints, it destroys the base in an explosion. Upon learning of the incident, Nerv HQ, under [[Gendo Ikari]]'s command, attempts to eject the entry plug and remotely deactivate the Eva. Once this fails, Eva-03 is officially designated the 13th Angel, and all three Evangelions under Nerv HQ's control are sent to intercept it. Eva-03 attacks and disables [[Evangelion Unit-02|Eva-02]] and [[Evangelion Unit-00|Eva-00]], and proceeds to assault [[Evangelion Unit-01|Eva-01]]. After Eva-01 is switched to [[Dummy System|dummy system]] control, Eva-03 is quickly overwhelmed and is destroyed by gruesome dismemberment. Eva-01 presumably destroys Eva-03's [[core]] off-screen, since it is seen pulling off the chest plate that covers and protects the organ.Eva-01 is actually depicted attacking the core in [[Neon Genesis Evangelion (manga)|Sadamoto's manga]]. ==Features== Eva-03's armor is a characteristic black (or midnight blue) with white trim. It is described as a Production Model Evangelion, although in terms of obvious physical similarities, it has more in common with the early Evas than the preceding Production Model, Eva-02. The head possesses two eyes (white with red irises), with four nostrils situated on the forehead (evidenced by the holes in the prominent nose piece). The Eva has red blood, and the skin is apparently, like most Evas, dark brown. The armor configuration is fully identical to that of Eva-04, and the same with the exception of the helmet design to Eva-02 and the upgraded Eva-00. As Eva-03 was parasitized by an Angel and "possessed" almost immediately upon activation, any new features it may have been designed with, such as weapons, remain unknown. Its arms are seen to gain unusually long length and super-elasticity as a result of the parasitism. ==Soul== The soul of Eva-03 is not explicitly mentioned in the series, but the general consensus is that it is the soul of Toji's mother. Other theories hold that it is his hospitalized sister, or yet another iteration of the Rei/Lilith soul. See '''[[Theory and Analysis:Eva-03's Soul|Eva-03's Soul]]''' for details.
{{MontageBox |float = left |columns = 4 |title = Evangelion Unit-03 |colour = |image1 = [[Image:18 C002 col-disc.jpg|thumb|200px|Suspended in transit.]] |image2 = [[Image:18 C004 clouds.jpg|thumb|200px|The transport aircraft carrying Eva-03 flying into cumulonimbus clouds.]] |image3 = [[Image:18 C132 strain.jpg|thumb|200px|Infected Eva-03 struggles to break from its restraints.]] |image4 = [[Image:18 C167 eva03-silhouette.jpg|thumb|200px|Approaching in silhouette. Note the unusually long arms.]] |image5 = [[Image:18 C224 stretch.jpg|thumb|200px|The super-elasticity of the parasitized Eva-03's arms.]] |image6 = [[Image:18 C279 headsplode.jpg|thumb|200px|Eva-03's head flying apart. (Note the eye and jaw.)]] |image7 = [[Image:18 C283 chestplate.jpg|thumb|200px|Eva-01 removing Eva-03's chest plate.]] |image8 = [[Image:19 C024 eva03-arm.jpg|thumb|200px|Arm with exposed flesh and bone.]] |image9 = |image10 = |caption = }} {{-}} ==Allusions== Toji's usual attire bears a striking resemblance to Eva-03's color scheme, perhaps foreshadowing (along with just about every other hint) that he is the pilot. In Rebuild, the similarity between Toji's attire and Eva-03's color scheme remains, even though Asuka, not Toji, serves as the test pilot. ==References== {{reflist}} {{Evangelion List}} [[Category:A to Z]] [[Category:Evangelions]]
html:
NGE NTE Anima Other

Evangelion Unit-03
エヴァンゲリオン３号機
Model Type Production
Pilot(s) Series: Toji Suzuhara
Soul(s) Series: Toji's mother
First Sortie Series: Episode 18 (activation test)

Evangelion Unit-03 (エヴァンゲリオン３号機, Evangerion Sangouki), built by Nerv's First Branch in the United States, is the second Production Model Evangelion.

Contents

  • 1 Profile
  • 2 Features
  • 3 Soul
  • 4 Allusions
  • 5 References

Profile[edit]

Following the incident resulting in the sudden disappearance of the Second Branch (Nevada) and Eva-03's sister unit, Eva-04, the U.S. government panics and demands that Eva-03 be transported off of its soil to Japan. Toji Suzuhara is selected to serve as the Eva's pilot and sent to Nerv's secondary testing facility at Matsushiro to conduct Eva-03's first activation test, supervised by Ritsuko Akagi and Misato Katsuragi.

While suspended from a transport plane during its flight to Japan, Eva-03 enters a series of cumulonimbus clouds, suspected to be the point at which it was infected by the 13th Angel, Bardiel[1]. During the activation experiment at the Matsushiro facility, Eva-03 goes berserk under, ostensibly, Bardiel's control, the parasitic "infection" manifest beneath the armor as slime fungus-like growths[2]. After briefly struggling to break free from its restraints, it destroys the base in an explosion.

Upon learning of the incident, Nerv HQ, under Gendo Ikari's command, attempts to eject the entry plug and remotely deactivate the Eva. Once this fails, Eva-03 is officially designated the 13th Angel, and all three Evangelions under Nerv HQ's control are sent to intercept it. Eva-03 attacks and disables Eva-02 and Eva-00, and proceeds to assault Eva-01. After Eva-01 is switched to dummy system control, Eva-03 is quickly overwhelmed and is destroyed by gruesome dismemberment. Eva-01 presumably destroys Eva-03's core off-screen, since it is seen pulling off the chest plate that covers and protects the organ.[3]

Features[edit]

Eva-03's armor is a characteristic black (or midnight blue) with white trim. It is described as a Production Model Evangelion, although in terms of obvious physical similarities, it has more in common with the early Evas than the preceding Production Model, Eva-02. The head possesses two eyes (white with red irises), with four nostrils situated on the forehead (evidenced by the holes in the prominent nose piece). The Eva has red blood, and the skin is apparently, like most Evas, dark brown. The armor configuration is fully identical to that of Eva-04, and the same with the exception of the helmet design to Eva-02 and the upgraded Eva-00.

As Eva-03 was parasitized by an Angel and "possessed" almost immediately upon activation, any new features it may have been designed with, such as weapons, remain unknown.

Its arms are seen to gain unusually long length and super-elasticity as a result of the parasitism.

Soul[edit]

The soul of Eva-03 is not explicitly mentioned in the series, but the general consensus is that it is the soul of Toji's mother.

Other theories hold that it is his hospitalized sister, or yet another iteration of the Rei/Lilith soul.

See Eva-03's Soul for details.


Evangelion Unit-03


Allusions[edit]

Toji's usual attire bears a striking resemblance to Eva-03's color scheme, perhaps foreshadowing (along with just about every other hint) that he is the pilot. In Rebuild, the similarity between Toji's attire and Eva-03's color scheme remains, even though Asuka, not Toji, serves as the test pilot.

References[edit]

  1. ↑ "Conceivably, Bardiel lurked within the cumulonimbus clouds and parasitized the in-transit Eva-03 when it passed through." (積乱雲の中に潜み、移送中の３号機が通過した際に寄生したものと考えられるバルディエル。) From Evangelion Chronicle, Mechanic Sheet 04: Evangelion Production Model / Thirteenth Angel Bardiel. Translation by Reichu.
  2. ↑ "Bardiel: The slime fungus-shaped Thirteenth Angel that parasitized Eva-03." (バルディエル：3号機に寄生した粘菌状の第十三使徒。) Neon Genesis Evangelion Newtype 100% Collection, p. 144. Translation by Reichu.
  3. ↑ Eva-01 is actually depicted attacking the core in Sadamoto's manga.


Evangelions
Evangelions (general)
Failed Evangelion Prototypes | Simulation Bodies
Eva-00 | Eva-01 | Eva-02 | Eva-03 | Eva-04 | Mass Production Evangelions (Evas 05~13)
Rebuild of Evangelion: Mark.04 | Eva-05 | Mark.06 | Eva-08 | Mark.09 | Eva-13
Eva 8+2 | Mass Production Evangelions | Failures of Infinity
Other: Extracanonical Evas

(積乱雲の中に潜み、移送中の３号機が通過した際に寄生したものと考えられるバルディエル。) From ''[[Evangelion Chronicle]]'', Mechanic Sheet 04: Evangelion Production Model / Thirteenth Angel Bardiel. Translation by [[User:Reichu|Reichu]].
"Bardiel: The slime fungus-shaped Thirteenth Angel that parasitized Eva-03." (バルディエル：3号機に寄生した粘菌状の第十三使徒。) ''Neon Genesis Evangelion Newtype 100% Collection'', p. 144. Translation by Reichu.
Eva-01 is actually depicted attacking the core in [[Neon Genesis Evangelion (manga)|Sadamoto's manga]].
